text: In this age of grace, the church remains God’s primary missionary (Matt 28:20). Through missions in an age of globalisation the church will make visible the divine love across ethnic and cultural dividing lines. Although evangelicals and pentecostals have frequently understood missio cross-cultural or cross-frontier in terms of evangelistic mission activities, commonly referred to as missio[1], based on Matthew 28:19–20 as the commissions stone, this wider biblical basis for describing the missions work of the local church here demonstrates more varied functions in missio in cross-cultural mission using Romans 15:22–33 for a thematic missiological analysis to examine how the church can take part through the sharing of power in cross-cultural missions through evangelism through accompanying and sending missionary workers directly into the mission field as well as supporting their missionary work with finances used to achieve a more holistic expression of the Gospel, that not only talks to humanity’s spiritual needs, but also its physical needs where the missionaries have to depend on prayers, so this study intends to represent the missionary work of the church in an in-depth, holistic way to represent missionary work among cross-cultural communities in our era.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
